figures.caption: Table 2. Performance (%) of LLMs and LLM-based enhanced methods on PlayEval. We report mean values over 5 independent runs with 95% confidence intervals calculated using the Student’s 𝑡-distribution.

Table 3. Performance (%) of PlayCoder compared to baselines across different LLMs. We report mean values over 5 independent runs with 95% confidence intervals calculated using the Student’s 𝑡-distribution.

Fig. 3. Testing a 2048 implementation. Left: the rendered 4 × 4 grid shows tiles at (3,1), (3,4), and (4,4) with values 2, 2, and 4. Right: the agent’s structured reasoning process including state analysis, coverage assessment, verification protocols, strategy selection, and exception-aware considerations that lead to a recommended rightward swipe.
Fig. 5. Case study of PlayCoder testing a 2048 game.
Fig. 2. The structure of PlayEval Data.
Table 6. Performance (%) of PlayCoder across different LLMs, 5 runs + 95% CI (t-distribution).

Table 4. Computational efficiency comparison across baselines and PlayCoder (GPT-5-mini as backbone LLM). Efficiency@k calculated as Play@k divided by tokens per function (×103). We report mean values over 5 independent runs with 95% confidence intervals calculated using the Student’s 𝑡-distribution.
